---
title: "Practice"
---

This page contains practice problems that span topics from the this section. For each type of problem, click **New Problem** to get another one, type your answer in the box, and click **Check** to see how you did. If you get stuck, click **Show Answer** to reveal a worked result.

```{=html}
<style>
  .practice-card {
    border: 1px solid var(--bs-border-color, #ccc);
    border-radius: 8px;
    padding: 1rem 1.25rem;
    margin: 1rem 0 1.5rem 0;
    background: var(--bs-tertiary-bg, #f8f9fa);
  }
  .practice-card .prompt {
    font-size: 1.05rem;
    margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
  }
  .practice-card .prompt code,
  .practice-card .answer-box code {
    font-size: 1rem;
  }
  .practice-card input[type="text"] {
    font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Consolas, monospace;
    padding: 0.4rem 0.6rem;
    border: 1px solid var(--bs-border-color, #ccc);
    border-radius: 4px;
    width: min(100%, 30rem);
    margin-right: 0.5rem;
  }
  .practice-card button {
    padding: 0.4rem 0.8rem;
    border: 1px solid var(--bs-border-color, #ccc);
    border-radius: 4px;
    background: var(--bs-body-bg, #fff);
    cursor: pointer;
    margin-right: 0.25rem;
  }
  .practice-card button:hover {
    background: var(--bs-secondary-bg, #e9ecef);
  }
  .practice-card .feedback {
    margin-top: 0.6rem;
    min-height: 1.2rem;
    font-weight: 500;
  }
  .practice-card .feedback.correct { color: #198754; }
  .practice-card .feedback.incorrect { color: #dc3545; }
  .practice-card .answer-box {
    margin-top: 0.6rem;
    padding: 0.6rem 0.8rem;
    background: var(--bs-body-bg, #fff);
    border-left: 3px solid #0d6efd;
    border-radius: 4px;
    font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Consolas, monospace;
    white-space: pre-wrap;
  }
  .practice-card .controls {
    display: flex;
    flex-wrap: wrap;
    gap: 0.25rem;
    align-items: center;
  }
  .practice-card .mc-options {
    margin: 0.5rem 0 0.75rem 0;
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: column;
    gap: 0.25rem;
  }
  .practice-card .mc-options label {
    display: flex;
    align-items: flex-start;
    gap: 0.5rem;
    padding: 0.3rem 0.5rem;
    border-radius: 4px;
    cursor: pointer;
  }
  .practice-card .mc-options label:hover {
    background: var(--bs-secondary-bg, #e9ecef);
  }
  .practice-card .mc-options input[type="radio"] {
    margin-top: 0.3rem;
  }
  .practice-card .data-table {
    border-collapse: collapse;
    margin: 0.4rem 0;
  }
  .practice-card .data-table td,
  .practice-card .data-table th {
    border: 1px solid var(--bs-border-color, #ccc);
    padding: 0.25rem 0.6rem;
    font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Consolas, monospace;
    text-align: center;
  }
</style>
```

## Base 10 → Two's Complement (8-bit)

Convert the given decimal integer to its **8-bit two's complement** binary representation. Spaces in your answer are ignored, so `10110011` and `1011 0011` are both accepted.

```{=html}
<div class="practice-card" id="tc-encode">
  <div class="prompt">Convert <code id="tc-encode-value">42</code> to 8-bit two's complement.</div>
  <div class="controls">
    <input type="text" id="tc-encode-input" placeholder="e.g. 0010 1010" autocomplete="off" />
    <button onclick="checkTcEncode()">Check</button>
    <button onclick="newTcEncode()">New Problem</button>
    <button onclick="showTcEncode()">Show Answer</button>
  </div>
  <div class="feedback" id="tc-encode-feedback"></div>
  <div class="answer-box" id="tc-encode-answer" style="display:none;"></div>
</div>
```

## Two's Complement → Base 10 (8-bit)

The given binary value is an **8-bit two's complement** number. Convert it to decimal (it may be negative).

```{=html}
<div class="practice-card" id="tc-decode">
  <div class="prompt">Convert <code id="tc-decode-value">1101 0110</code> from 8-bit two's complement to decimal.</div>
  <div class="controls">
    <input type="text" id="tc-decode-input" placeholder="e.g. -42" autocomplete="off" />
    <button onclick="checkTcDecode()">Check</button>
    <button onclick="newTcDecode()">New Problem</button>
    <button onclick="showTcDecode()">Show Answer</button>
  </div>
  <div class="feedback" id="tc-decode-feedback"></div>
  <div class="answer-box" id="tc-decode-answer" style="display:none;"></div>
</div>
```

## Base 10 → IEEE 754 Single Precision

Convert the given decimal value to its **32-bit IEEE 754 single precision** binary representation (in normalized form). Spaces are ignored. You may give the full 32 bits as one string, or grouped as `S Exponent Mantissa`.

```{=html}
<div class="practice-card" id="ieee-encode">
  <div class="prompt">Convert <code id="ieee-encode-value">-4.25</code> to single precision IEEE 754.</div>
  <div class="controls">
    <input type="text" id="ieee-encode-input" placeholder="32 bits, e.g. 1 10000001 00010000000000000000000" autocomplete="off" />
    <button onclick="checkIeeeEncode()">Check</button>
    <button onclick="newIeeeEncode()">New Problem</button>
    <button onclick="showIeeeEncode()">Show Answer</button>
  </div>
  <div class="feedback" id="ieee-encode-feedback"></div>
  <div class="answer-box" id="ieee-encode-answer" style="display:none;"></div>
</div>
```

## IEEE 754 Single Precision → Base 10

The given 32 bits represent a **single precision IEEE 754** value (in normalized form). Convert it to decimal.

```{=html}
<div class="practice-card" id="ieee-decode">
  <div class="prompt">Convert the value below to decimal.</div>
  <div class="answer-box" id="ieee-decode-value" style="border-left-color:#6c757d;"></div>
  <div class="controls" style="margin-top:0.6rem;">
    <input type="text" id="ieee-decode-input" placeholder="e.g. -4.25" autocomplete="off" />
    <button onclick="checkIeeeDecode()">Check</button>
    <button onclick="newIeeeDecode()">New Problem</button>
    <button onclick="showIeeeDecode()">Show Answer</button>
  </div>
  <div class="feedback" id="ieee-decode-feedback"></div>
  <div class="answer-box" id="ieee-decode-answer" style="display:none;"></div>
</div>
```

## Decoder Output

Recall: an *n*-to-2^*n*^ **decoder** raises the single output line whose index matches the unsigned binary input. Given the decoder size and input pattern below, identify which output line is high.

```{=html}
<div class="practice-card" id="dec-out">
  <div class="prompt" id="dec-out-prompt"></div>
  <div class="controls">
    <input type="text" id="dec-out-input" placeholder="output line number, e.g. 5" autocomplete="off" />
    <button onclick="checkDecOut()">Check</button>
    <button onclick="newDecOut()">New Problem</button>
    <button onclick="showDecOut()">Show Answer</button>
  </div>
  <div class="feedback" id="dec-out-feedback"></div>
  <div class="answer-box" id="dec-out-answer" style="display:none;"></div>
</div>
```

## Encoder Output

Recall: a 2^*n*^-to-*n* **encoder** outputs the *n*-bit unsigned binary number that corresponds to the single high input line. Given which input line is high, give the encoder's output bits.

```{=html}
<div class="practice-card" id="enc-out">
  <div class="prompt" id="enc-out-prompt"></div>
  <div class="controls">
    <input type="text" id="enc-out-input" placeholder="binary, e.g. 101" autocomplete="off" />
    <button onclick="checkEncOut()">Check</button>
    <button onclick="newEncOut()">New Problem</button>
    <button onclick="showEncOut()">Show Answer</button>
  </div>
  <div class="feedback" id="enc-out-feedback"></div>
  <div class="answer-box" id="enc-out-answer" style="display:none;"></div>
</div>
```

## Multiplexer Output

Recall: a multiplexer (MUX) routes the value of the data line selected by its selector bits to the single output. Below are the data line values and selector bits. Give the value at the MUX output.

```{=html}
<div class="practice-card" id="mux-out">
  <div class="prompt" id="mux-out-prompt"></div>
  <div id="mux-out-table"></div>
  <div class="controls">
    <input type="text" id="mux-out-input" placeholder="0 or 1" autocomplete="off" />
    <button onclick="checkMuxOut()">Check</button>
    <button onclick="newMuxOut()">New Problem</button>
    <button onclick="showMuxOut()">Show Answer</button>
  </div>
  <div class="feedback" id="mux-out-feedback"></div>
  <div class="answer-box" id="mux-out-answer" style="display:none;"></div>
</div>
```

## Demultiplexer Output

Recall: a demultiplexer (DEMUX) copies its single data input *A* to the output line chosen by the selector bits; all other outputs are 0. Given the selector bits, identify which output line carries the input value.

```{=html}
<div class="practice-card" id="demux-out">
  <div class="prompt" id="demux-out-prompt"></div>
  <div class="controls">
    <input type="text" id="demux-out-input" placeholder="output line number, e.g. 2" autocomplete="off" />
    <button onclick="checkDemuxOut()">Check</button>
    <button onclick="newDemuxOut()">New Problem</button>
    <button onclick="showDemuxOut()">Show Answer</button>
  </div>
  <div class="feedback" id="demux-out-feedback"></div>
  <div class="answer-box" id="demux-out-answer" style="display:none;"></div>
</div>
```

## R-S Flip-Flop Next State

Recall the R-S flip-flop truth table: `R=0,S=0` holds the current state, `R=0,S=1` sets *Q* to 1, `R=1,S=0` resets *Q* to 0, and `R=1,S=1` is disallowed. Given the current state and inputs, give the next *Q*.

```{=html}
<div class="practice-card" id="rs-next">
  <div class="prompt" id="rs-next-prompt"></div>
  <div class="controls">
    <input type="text" id="rs-next-input" placeholder="0 or 1" autocomplete="off" />
    <button onclick="checkRsNext()">Check</button>
    <button onclick="newRsNext()">New Problem</button>
    <button onclick="showRsNext()">Show Answer</button>
  </div>
  <div class="feedback" id="rs-next-feedback"></div>
  <div class="answer-box" id="rs-next-answer" style="display:none;"></div>
</div>
```

## Instruction Cycle

The five-step instruction cycle is the heart of every register-based computer. Pick the correct answer to the question below.

```{=html}
<div class="practice-card" id="instr-cycle">
  <div class="prompt" id="instr-cycle-prompt"></div>
  <div class="mc-options" id="instr-cycle-options"></div>
  <div class="controls">
    <button onclick="checkInstrCycle()">Check</button>
    <button onclick="newInstrCycle()">New Problem</button>
    <button onclick="showInstrCycle()">Show Answer</button>
  </div>
  <div class="feedback" id="instr-cycle-feedback"></div>
  <div class="answer-box" id="instr-cycle-answer" style="display:none;"></div>
</div>
```

## Definitions

Match the term to its definition (or the definition to its term). 

```{=html}
<div class="practice-card" id="defs">
  <div class="prompt" id="defs-prompt"></div>
  <div class="mc-options" id="defs-options"></div>
  <div class="controls">
    <button onclick="checkDefs()">Check</button>
    <button onclick="newDefs()">New Problem</button>
    <button onclick="showDefs()">Show Answer</button>
  </div>
  <div class="feedback" id="defs-feedback"></div>
  <div class="answer-box" id="defs-answer" style="display:none;"></div>
</div>
```

```{=html}
<script>
  // ---------- Helpers ----------
  function stripBin(s) { return (s || "").replace(/\s+/g, ""); }
  function isBinary(s, len) {
    return s.length === len && /^[01]+$/.test(s);
  }
  function groupBits(bits, n) {
    const out = [];
    for (let i = 0; i < bits.length; i += n) out.push(bits.slice(i, i + n));
    return out.join(" ");
  }
  function randInt(lo, hi) { // inclusive
    return Math.floor(Math.random() * (hi - lo + 1)) + lo;
  }
  function setFeedback(id, text, ok) {
    const el = document.getElementById(id);
    el.textContent = text;
    el.className = "feedback " + (ok === true ? "correct" : ok === false ? "incorrect" : "");
  }

  // ---------- Two's Complement (8-bit) ----------
  function toTwosComplement8(n) {
    if (n < 0) n = 256 + n;
    return n.toString(2).padStart(8, "0");
  }
  function fromTwosComplement8(bits) {
    let n = parseInt(bits, 2);
    if (n >= 128) n -= 256;
    return n;
  }

  // --- Base 10 -> Two's Complement
  let tcEncodeAnswer = "";
  function newTcEncode() {
    const value = randInt(-128, 127);
    document.getElementById("tc-encode-value").textContent = value;
    tcEncodeAnswer = toTwosComplement8(value);
    document.getElementById("tc-encode-input").value = "";
    document.getElementById("tc-encode-answer").style.display = "none";
    setFeedback("tc-encode-feedback", "", null);
  }
  function checkTcEncode() {
    const guess = stripBin(document.getElementById("tc-encode-input").value);
    if (!isBinary(guess, 8)) {
      setFeedback("tc-encode-feedback", "Please enter exactly 8 binary digits (0 or 1).", false);
      return;
    }
    if (guess === tcEncodeAnswer) {
      setFeedback("tc-encode-feedback", "Correct! " + groupBits(tcEncodeAnswer, 4), true);
    } else {
      setFeedback("tc-encode-feedback", "Not quite. Try again, or click Show Answer.", false);
    }
  }
  function showTcEncode() {
    const value = document.getElementById("tc-encode-value").textContent;
    const box = document.getElementById("tc-encode-answer");
    box.style.display = "block";
    box.textContent =
`${value} in 8-bit two's complement is:
  ${groupBits(tcEncodeAnswer, 4)}`;
  }

  // --- Two's Complement -> Base 10
  let tcDecodeAnswer = 0;
  function newTcDecode() {
    const bits = toTwosComplement8(randInt(-128, 127));
    document.getElementById("tc-decode-value").textContent = groupBits(bits, 4);
    tcDecodeAnswer = fromTwosComplement8(bits);
    document.getElementById("tc-decode-input").value = "";
    document.getElementById("tc-decode-answer").style.display = "none";
    setFeedback("tc-decode-feedback", "", null);
  }
  function checkTcDecode() {
    const raw = document.getElementById("tc-decode-input").value.trim();
    if (!/^-?\d+$/.test(raw)) {
      setFeedback("tc-decode-feedback", "Please enter an integer (e.g. -42 or 17).", false);
      return;
    }
    if (parseInt(raw, 10) === tcDecodeAnswer) {
      setFeedback("tc-decode-feedback", "Correct!", true);
    } else {
      setFeedback("tc-decode-feedback", "Not quite. Try again, or click Show Answer.", false);
    }
  }
  function showTcDecode() {
    const bits = stripBin(document.getElementById("tc-decode-value").textContent);
    const box = document.getElementById("tc-decode-answer");
    box.style.display = "block";
    if (tcDecodeAnswer >= 0) {
      box.textContent =
`The leading bit is 0, so the value is positive.
Reading ${groupBits(bits, 4)} as an unsigned binary number:
  = ${tcDecodeAnswer}`;
    } else {
      const positiveBits = toTwosComplement8(-tcDecodeAnswer);
      box.textContent =
`The leading bit is 1, so the value is negative.
Flip the bits and add 1 to find the magnitude:
  start:    ${groupBits(bits, 4)}
  flipped:  ${groupBits(bits.split("").map(b => b === "0" ? "1" : "0").join(""), 4)}
  + 1   :   ${groupBits(positiveBits, 4)}  =  ${-tcDecodeAnswer}
Apply the negative sign:  ${tcDecodeAnswer}`;
    }
  }

  // ---------- IEEE 754 Single Precision ----------
  function floatToIEEE754(f) {
    const buf = new ArrayBuffer(4);
    new Float32Array(buf)[0] = f;
    const i = new Uint32Array(buf)[0];
    return i.toString(2).padStart(32, "0");
  }
  function ieee754ToFloat(bits) {
    const i = parseInt(bits, 2);
    const buf = new ArrayBuffer(4);
    new Uint32Array(buf)[0] = i >>> 0;
    return new Float32Array(buf)[0];
  }
  function formatIEEE(bits) {
    const s = bits.slice(0, 1);
    const e = bits.slice(1, 9);
    const m = bits.slice(9);
    return `S    Exponent    Mantissa (23 bits total)
--   ----------  ----------------------------
${s}    ${e.slice(0,4)} ${e.slice(4)}   ${m.slice(0,4)} ${m.slice(4,8)} ${m.slice(8,12)} ${m.slice(12,16)} ${m.slice(16,20)} ${m.slice(20)}`;
  }

  // Generate a "clean" float that has a finite, short binary expansion in normalized form.
  // Strategy: pick a random sign, an integer numerator, and a power-of-two denominator.
  function generateCleanFloat() {
    const sign = Math.random() < 0.5 ? -1 : 1;
    // Numerator chosen so the value is comfortably in the normalized range and not 0.
    let num = randInt(1, 511);          // up to 9 significant bits
    const denomPow = randInt(0, 4);     // /1, /2, /4, /8, or /16
    const denom = 1 << denomPow;
    return sign * (num / denom);
  }

  // --- Base 10 -> IEEE 754
  let ieeeEncodeAnswer = "";
  function newIeeeEncode() {
    const value = generateCleanFloat();
    ieeeEncodeAnswer = floatToIEEE754(value);
    document.getElementById("ieee-encode-value").textContent = value;
    document.getElementById("ieee-encode-input").value = "";
    document.getElementById("ieee-encode-answer").style.display = "none";
    setFeedback("ieee-encode-feedback", "", null);
  }
  function checkIeeeEncode() {
    const guess = stripBin(document.getElementById("ieee-encode-input").value);
    if (!isBinary(guess, 32)) {
      setFeedback("ieee-encode-feedback", "Please enter exactly 32 binary digits (0 or 1). Spaces are OK.", false);
      return;
    }
    if (guess === ieeeEncodeAnswer) {
      setFeedback("ieee-encode-feedback", "Correct!", true);
    } else {
      setFeedback("ieee-encode-feedback", "Not quite. Try again, or click Show Answer.", false);
    }
  }
  function showIeeeEncode() {
    const value = document.getElementById("ieee-encode-value").textContent;
    const box = document.getElementById("ieee-encode-answer");
    box.style.display = "block";
    box.textContent = `${value} in single precision IEEE 754:\n\n${formatIEEE(ieeeEncodeAnswer)}\n\nRaw 32 bits: ${ieeeEncodeAnswer}`;
  }

  // --- IEEE 754 -> Base 10
  let ieeeDecodeAnswer = 0;
  let ieeeDecodeBits = "";
  function newIeeeDecode() {
    const value = generateCleanFloat();
    ieeeDecodeBits = floatToIEEE754(value);
    ieeeDecodeAnswer = ieee754ToFloat(ieeeDecodeBits);
    document.getElementById("ieee-decode-value").textContent = formatIEEE(ieeeDecodeBits);
    document.getElementById("ieee-decode-input").value = "";
    document.getElementById("ieee-decode-answer").style.display = "none";
    setFeedback("ieee-decode-feedback", "", null);
  }
  function checkIeeeDecode() {
    const raw = document.getElementById("ieee-decode-input").value.trim();
    const num = Number(raw);
    if (raw === "" || Number.isNaN(num)) {
      setFeedback("ieee-decode-feedback", "Please enter a decimal number (e.g. -4.25).", false);
      return;
    }
    // Compare with a tiny tolerance to allow for rounding in student input.
    const tol = Math.max(1e-6, Math.abs(ieeeDecodeAnswer) * 1e-6);
    if (Math.abs(num - ieeeDecodeAnswer) <= tol) {
      setFeedback("ieee-decode-feedback", "Correct!", true);
    } else {
      setFeedback("ieee-decode-feedback", "Not quite. Try again, or click Show Answer.", false);
    }
  }
  function showIeeeDecode() {
    const box = document.getElementById("ieee-decode-answer");
    box.style.display = "block";
    const sign = ieeeDecodeBits[0];
    const expBits = ieeeDecodeBits.slice(1, 9);
    const mantBits = ieeeDecodeBits.slice(9).replace(/0+$/, "") || "0";
    const biased = parseInt(expBits, 2);
    const trueExp = biased - 127;
    box.textContent =
`Sign bit: ${sign}  ->  ${sign === "0" ? "+" : "-"}
Exponent: ${expBits} = ${biased},  unbiased = ${biased} - 127 = ${trueExp}
Mantissa (without trailing zeros): ${mantBits}

Value = (-1)^${sign} x 1.${mantBits} x 2^${trueExp}
      = ${ieeeDecodeAnswer}`;
  }

  // ---------- Multiple-choice helpers ----------
  function shuffle(arr) {
    const a = arr.slice();
    for (let i = a.length - 1; i > 0; i--) {
      const j = Math.floor(Math.random() * (i + 1));
      [a[i], a[j]] = [a[j], a[i]];
    }
    return a;
  }
  function pickRandom(arr) {
    return arr[Math.floor(Math.random() * arr.length)];
  }
  function pickDistinct(arr, k, excludePred) {
    const pool = arr.filter(x => !excludePred || !excludePred(x));
    return shuffle(pool).slice(0, k);
  }
  function renderMC(containerId, name, optionsText) {
    const cont = document.getElementById(containerId);
    cont.innerHTML = "";
    optionsText.forEach((text, i) => {
      const lbl = document.createElement("label");
      const radio = document.createElement("input");
      radio.type = "radio";
      radio.name = name;
      radio.value = String(i);
      const span = document.createElement("span");
      span.textContent = text;
      lbl.appendChild(radio);
      lbl.appendChild(span);
      cont.appendChild(lbl);
    });
  }
  function getMCSelected(name) {
    const sel = document.querySelector(`input[name="${name}"]:checked`);
    return sel ? parseInt(sel.value, 10) : -1;
  }

  // ---------- Decoder Output ----------
  let decOutN = 0, decOutValue = 0;
  function newDecOut() {
    decOutN = randInt(2, 4);
    decOutValue = randInt(0, (1 << decOutN) - 1);
    const bits = decOutValue.toString(2).padStart(decOutN, "0");
    document.getElementById("dec-out-prompt").innerHTML =
      `An <strong>${decOutN}-to-${1 << decOutN}</strong> decoder receives input ` +
      `A<sub>${decOutN-1}</sub>...A<sub>0</sub> = <code>${bits}</code>. ` +
      `Which output line D<sub>x</sub> goes high? (Enter the index x.)`;
    document.getElementById("dec-out-input").value = "";
    document.getElementById("dec-out-answer").style.display = "none";
    setFeedback("dec-out-feedback", "", null);
  }
  function checkDecOut() {
    const raw = document.getElementById("dec-out-input").value.trim();
    if (!/^\d+$/.test(raw)) {
      setFeedback("dec-out-feedback", "Please enter a non-negative integer.", false);
      return;
    }
    if (parseInt(raw, 10) === decOutValue) {
      setFeedback("dec-out-feedback", "Correct!", true);
    } else {
      setFeedback("dec-out-feedback", "Not quite. Try again, or click Show Answer.", false);
    }
  }
  function showDecOut() {
    const bits = decOutValue.toString(2).padStart(decOutN, "0");
    const box = document.getElementById("dec-out-answer");
    box.style.display = "block";
    box.textContent =
`Input ${bits} (binary) = ${decOutValue} (decimal).
Output line D${decOutValue} is set to 1; all other outputs are 0.`;
  }

  // ---------- Encoder Output ----------
  let encOutN = 0, encOutLine = 0;
  function newEncOut() {
    encOutN = randInt(2, 4);
    encOutLine = randInt(0, (1 << encOutN) - 1);
    document.getElementById("enc-out-prompt").innerHTML =
      `In a <strong>${1 << encOutN}-to-${encOutN}</strong> encoder, only line ` +
      `D<sub>${encOutLine}</sub> is high (all other inputs are 0). ` +
      `What ${encOutN}-bit value does the encoder output?`;
    document.getElementById("enc-out-input").value = "";
    document.getElementById("enc-out-answer").style.display = "none";
    setFeedback("enc-out-feedback", "", null);
  }
  function checkEncOut() {
    const guess = stripBin(document.getElementById("enc-out-input").value);
    const expected = encOutLine.toString(2).padStart(encOutN, "0");
    if (!isBinary(guess, encOutN)) {
      setFeedback("enc-out-feedback", `Please enter exactly ${encOutN} binary digits (0 or 1).`, false);
      return;
    }
    if (guess === expected) {
      setFeedback("enc-out-feedback", "Correct!", true);
    } else {
      setFeedback("enc-out-feedback", "Not quite. Try again, or click Show Answer.", false);
    }
  }
  function showEncOut() {
    const expected = encOutLine.toString(2).padStart(encOutN, "0");
    const box = document.getElementById("enc-out-answer");
    box.style.display = "block";
    box.textContent =
`Line D${encOutLine} is high, so the encoder outputs the ${encOutN}-bit unsigned binary representation of ${encOutLine}:
  ${expected}`;
  }

  // ---------- Multiplexer Output ----------
  let muxOutN = 0, muxOutData = [], muxOutSel = 0;
  function newMuxOut() {
    muxOutN = randInt(1, 3);
    const numLines = 1 << muxOutN;
    muxOutData = Array.from({length: numLines}, () => randInt(0, 1));
    muxOutSel = randInt(0, numLines - 1);
    const selBits = muxOutSel.toString(2).padStart(muxOutN, "0");
    document.getElementById("mux-out-prompt").innerHTML =
      `A <strong>${numLines}-input multiplexer</strong> has selector bits ` +
      `S<sub>${muxOutN-1}</sub>...S<sub>0</sub> = <code>${selBits}</code>. ` +
      `The data lines hold the values shown below. What does the MUX output?`;
    let html = "<table class='data-table'><tr>";
    for (let i = numLines - 1; i >= 0; i--) html += `<th>D<sub>${i}</sub></th>`;
    html += "</tr><tr>";
    for (let i = numLines - 1; i >= 0; i--) html += `<td>${muxOutData[i]}</td>`;
    html += "</tr></table>";
    document.getElementById("mux-out-table").innerHTML = html;
    document.getElementById("mux-out-input").value = "";
    document.getElementById("mux-out-answer").style.display = "none";
    setFeedback("mux-out-feedback", "", null);
  }
  function checkMuxOut() {
    const raw = document.getElementById("mux-out-input").value.trim();
    if (raw !== "0" && raw !== "1") {
      setFeedback("mux-out-feedback", "Please enter 0 or 1.", false);
      return;
    }
    if (parseInt(raw, 10) === muxOutData[muxOutSel]) {
      setFeedback("mux-out-feedback", "Correct!", true);
    } else {
      setFeedback("mux-out-feedback", "Not quite. Try again, or click Show Answer.", false);
    }
  }
  function showMuxOut() {
    const selBits = muxOutSel.toString(2).padStart(muxOutN, "0");
    const box = document.getElementById("mux-out-answer");
    box.style.display = "block";
    box.textContent =
`Selector bits ${selBits} = ${muxOutSel} (decimal), so the MUX listens to D${muxOutSel}.
D${muxOutSel} = ${muxOutData[muxOutSel]}, so the output is ${muxOutData[muxOutSel]}.`;
  }

  // ---------- Demultiplexer Output ----------
  let demuxN = 0, demuxSel = 0;
  function newDemuxOut() {
    demuxN = randInt(2, 3);
    const numOut = 1 << demuxN;
    demuxSel = randInt(0, numOut - 1);
    const selBits = demuxSel.toString(2).padStart(demuxN, "0");
    document.getElementById("demux-out-prompt").innerHTML =
      `A <strong>${numOut}-output demultiplexer</strong> has selector bits ` +
      `S<sub>${demuxN-1}</sub>...S<sub>0</sub> = <code>${selBits}</code>. ` +
      `Which output line D<sub>x</sub> does the input A get routed to? (Enter the index x.)`;
    document.getElementById("demux-out-input").value = "";
    document.getElementById("demux-out-answer").style.display = "none";
    setFeedback("demux-out-feedback", "", null);
  }
  function checkDemuxOut() {
    const raw = document.getElementById("demux-out-input").value.trim();
    if (!/^\d+$/.test(raw)) {
      setFeedback("demux-out-feedback", "Please enter a non-negative integer.", false);
      return;
    }
    if (parseInt(raw, 10) === demuxSel) {
      setFeedback("demux-out-feedback", "Correct!", true);
    } else {
      setFeedback("demux-out-feedback", "Not quite. Try again, or click Show Answer.", false);
    }
  }
  function showDemuxOut() {
    const selBits = demuxSel.toString(2).padStart(demuxN, "0");
    const box = document.getElementById("demux-out-answer");
    box.style.display = "block";
    box.textContent =
`Selector bits ${selBits} = ${demuxSel} (decimal).
The input A is routed to D${demuxSel}; all other outputs stay at 0.`;
  }

  // ---------- R-S Flip-Flop ----------
  let rsCurQ = 0, rsR = 0, rsS = 0;
  function rsNextQ(curQ, r, s) {
    if (r === 1 && s === 1) return null; // disallowed
    if (r === 0 && s === 0) return curQ;
    if (s === 1) return 1;
    return 0;
  }
  function newRsNext() {
    rsCurQ = randInt(0, 1);
    // Pick (R,S) from {(0,0),(0,1),(1,0)} — exclude (1,1).
    const pairs = [[0,0],[0,1],[1,0]];
    const p = pickRandom(pairs);
    rsR = p[0]; rsS = p[1];
    document.getElementById("rs-next-prompt").innerHTML =
      `An R-S flip-flop currently holds Q = <code>${rsCurQ}</code>. ` +
      `The inputs are R = <code>${rsR}</code> and S = <code>${rsS}</code>. ` +
      `What is the next value of Q?`;
    document.getElementById("rs-next-input").value = "";
    document.getElementById("rs-next-answer").style.display = "none";
    setFeedback("rs-next-feedback", "", null);
  }
  function checkRsNext() {
    const raw = document.getElementById("rs-next-input").value.trim();
    if (raw !== "0" && raw !== "1") {
      setFeedback("rs-next-feedback", "Please enter 0 or 1.", false);
      return;
    }
    const next = rsNextQ(rsCurQ, rsR, rsS);
    if (parseInt(raw, 10) === next) {
      setFeedback("rs-next-feedback", "Correct!", true);
    } else {
      setFeedback("rs-next-feedback", "Not quite. Try again, or click Show Answer.", false);
    }
  }
  function showRsNext() {
    const next = rsNextQ(rsCurQ, rsR, rsS);
    const box = document.getElementById("rs-next-answer");
    box.style.display = "block";
    let reason = "";
    if (rsR === 0 && rsS === 0) reason = "R = 0 and S = 0, so the flip-flop holds its current state.";
    else if (rsR === 1 && rsS === 0) reason = "R = 1 forces a reset, so Q goes to 0.";
    else if (rsR === 0 && rsS === 1) reason = "S = 1 forces a set, so Q goes to 1.";
    box.textContent =
`${reason}
Next Q = ${next}.`;
  }

  // ---------- Instruction Cycle ----------
  const INSTR_STEPS = [
    "Fetch the next instruction from memory (using the program counter).",
    "Increment the program counter by 1.",
    "Decode the current instruction (identify op-code and operands).",
    "Execute the instruction (perform the operation in hardware).",
    "Return to step 1."
  ];
  const INSTR_QUESTIONS = [
    () => {
      const idx = randInt(0, 4);
      return {
        prompt: `Which of the following is <strong>step ${idx+1}</strong> of the instruction cycle?`,
        correct: INSTR_STEPS[idx],
        distractors: INSTR_STEPS.filter((_, i) => i !== idx)
      };
    },
    () => {
      const idx = randInt(0, 3); // ask "what comes after step idx"
      return {
        prompt: `Which step comes <strong>immediately after</strong> "${INSTR_STEPS[idx]}"?`,
        correct: INSTR_STEPS[idx + 1],
        distractors: INSTR_STEPS.filter((_, i) => i !== idx + 1)
      };
    },
 
  ];

  let instrCorrectIdx = 0, instrCorrectText = "";
  function newInstrCycle() {
    const q = INSTR_QUESTIONS[randInt(0, INSTR_QUESTIONS.length - 1)]();
    document.getElementById("instr-cycle-prompt").innerHTML = q.prompt;
    const distractors = pickDistinct(q.distractors, Math.min(3, q.distractors.length));
    const all = shuffle([q.correct, ...distractors]);
    instrCorrectIdx = all.indexOf(q.correct);
    instrCorrectText = q.correct;
    renderMC("instr-cycle-options", "instr-cycle-mc", all);
    document.getElementById("instr-cycle-answer").style.display = "none";
    setFeedback("instr-cycle-feedback", "", null);
  }
  function checkInstrCycle() {
    const sel = getMCSelected("instr-cycle-mc");
    if (sel < 0) {
      setFeedback("instr-cycle-feedback", "Please select an answer.", false);
      return;
    }
    if (sel === instrCorrectIdx) {
      setFeedback("instr-cycle-feedback", "Correct!", true);
    } else {
      setFeedback("instr-cycle-feedback", "Not quite. Try again, or click Show Answer.", false);
    }
  }
  function showInstrCycle() {
    const box = document.getElementById("instr-cycle-answer");
    box.style.display = "block";
    box.textContent = `Correct answer: ${instrCorrectText}`;
  }

  // ---------- Definitions ----------
  const DEFINITIONS = [
    {term: "decoder", def: "A circuit that takes an unsigned binary number on its input lines and raises the single output line whose index matches that number."},
    {term: "encoder", def: "A circuit with 2^n input lines (only one high at a time) that produces the n-bit unsigned binary number corresponding to the raised input line."},
    {term: "multiplexer (MUX)", def: "A circuit that transfers the value of one selected input data line to its single output line, based on the selector bits."},
    {term: "demultiplexer (DEMUX)", def: "A circuit with one data input, n selector lines, and 2^n output lines that copies the input value to the selected output and holds the others at 0."},
    {term: "flip-flop", def: "A device with two stable states, used to store a single bit."},
    {term: "sequential circuit", def: "A circuit whose outputs depend on its current inputs and its previous outputs (i.e., it incorporates feedback / memory)."},
    {term: "combinational circuit", def: "A digital circuit whose outputs depend only on its current inputs, with no feedback."},
    {term: "clock", def: "A device that generates a signal that periodically cycles between a high state and a low state."},
    {term: "clock cycle", def: "The interval of time beginning when the clock goes high, lasting through the return to low, and ending with the start of the transition back to high."},
    {term: "register", def: "A small, fast storage location inside the CPU that can be directly manipulated by its arithmetic and logic units."},
    {term: "word", def: "The base unit of data that is supported by a CPU."},
    {term: "op-code", def: "The part of a machine language instruction that defines what operation is to be performed."},
    {term: "operand", def: "The part of a machine language instruction that the op-code is applied to."},
    {term: "instruction register", def: "The special-purpose register that holds a copy of the instruction the CPU is currently executing."},
    {term: "program counter", def: "The special-purpose register that holds the address of the next instruction to execute."},
    {term: "status bits", def: "Bits that hold information about the most recently performed computation, such as carry, zero, negative, or overflow."},
    {term: "ALU", def: "The component of the CPU responsible for performing arithmetic and logic operations."},
    {term: "architecture", def: "The general organization of a machine."},
    {term: "overflow", def: "A condition that occurs when a value to be stored is outside the range of permissible values for the available bits."},
    {term: "two's complement", def: "A signed-integer encoding with a single representation for zero, in which negation is performed by flipping the bits and adding one."},

  ];
  let defsCorrectIdx = 0, defsCorrectText = "", defsTermAsked = "";
  function newDefs() {
    const askForDef = Math.random() < 0.5;
    const correct = pickRandom(DEFINITIONS);
    const distractors = pickDistinct(DEFINITIONS, 3, x => x.term === correct.term);
    let promptText, optionsText, correctText;
    if (askForDef) {
      promptText = `Which of the following is the best definition of <strong>${correct.term}</strong>?`;
      optionsText = shuffle([correct.def, ...distractors.map(d => d.def)]);
      correctText = correct.def;
    } else {
      promptText = `Which term best matches this definition: <em>"${correct.def}"</em>?`;
      optionsText = shuffle([correct.term, ...distractors.map(d => d.term)]);
      correctText = correct.term;
    }
    defsCorrectIdx = optionsText.indexOf(correctText);
    defsCorrectText = correctText;
    defsTermAsked = correct.term;
    document.getElementById("defs-prompt").innerHTML = promptText;
    renderMC("defs-options", "defs-mc", optionsText);
    document.getElementById("defs-answer").style.display = "none";
    setFeedback("defs-feedback", "", null);
  }
  function checkDefs() {
    const sel = getMCSelected("defs-mc");
    if (sel < 0) {
      setFeedback("defs-feedback", "Please select an answer.", false);
      return;
    }
    if (sel === defsCorrectIdx) {
      setFeedback("defs-feedback", "Correct!", true);
    } else {
      setFeedback("defs-feedback", "Not quite. Try again, or click Show Answer.", false);
    }
  }
  function showDefs() {
    const box = document.getElementById("defs-answer");
    box.style.display = "block";
    box.textContent = `Correct answer: ${defsCorrectText}`;
  }

  // ---------- Initialize all problems on page load ----------
  document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", function() {
    newTcEncode();
    newTcDecode();
    newIeeeEncode();
    newIeeeDecode();
    newDecOut();
    newEncOut();
    newMuxOut();
    newDemuxOut();
    newRsNext();
    newInstrCycle();
    newDefs();
  });
</script>
```
